identity.short_name: Count bounded cubics with exactly one double root and both roots in (-2,2)

subdomain(s): rational root theorem and Vieta relations
topic_path: counting coefficient triples by multiplicity and root-location constraints

problem_spec.summary: Count monic cubic polynomials with coefficients in a finite integer box that have exactly two distinct real roots (a double root and a simple root), and require both distinct roots to lie in a prescribed open interval. Use the repeated-root criterion to force a rational (hence integer) double root, then apply Vieta to pin down the remaining root and count admissible root pairs.
givens: Requirement: P has exactly two distinct real roots, both lying in (-2,2) (so P has one double real root and one simple real root).
ask: Find the number of such polynomials P.

parameter(s).name: target_interval
parameter(s).purpose: required location of the distinct real roots.

parameter(s).name: multiplicity_pattern
parameter(s).purpose: enforces exactly two distinct roots for a cubic.

invariant(s).name: multiplicity_characterization
invariant(s).statement: A real monic cubic has exactly two distinct real roots iff it factors as (x-r)^2(x-s) with r\neqs real, equivalently iff gcd(P,P′) has degree 1 (or discriminant \Delta=0 but P not a perfect cube).

invariant(s).name: double_root_rational_then_integer
invariant(s).statement: The double root r is a root of gcd(P,P′), hence rational; since P is monic with integer coefficients, the Rational Root Theorem forces r to be an integer dividing c.

invariant(s).name: interval_forces_small_root_set
invariant(s).statement: With r integer and r\in(-2,2), we must have r\in{-1,0,1}. Vieta then gives s=-a-2r, so s is also an integer and s\in(-2,2) forces s\in{-1,0,1}.

invariant(s).name: coefficient_reconstruction_from_roots
invariant(s).statement: If P(x)=(x-r)^2(x-s), then (a,b,c)=(-(2r+s), r^2+2rs, -r^2 s). Thus each admissible ordered pair (r,s) yields exactly one coefficient triple.

essential_assumption(s): The interval is open; roots equal to ±2 are excluded.
